title: spergularia media subsp. sauvagei
intro: Spergularia media subsp. sauvagei, also known as European sand-spurrey, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Spergularia media subsp. sauvagei include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.
